get.description:
Retrieves all active subscription plans with their features and pricing details.

**Public Access**: Can be called without authentication to display pricing page.

**Authenticated Access**: When called with a valid JWT token (shopId in context), 
the response includes additional flags:
- `isCurrentPlan`: True if this is the shop's active plan
- `canUpgrade`: True if the shop can upgrade to this plan

**Business Rules**:
- Upgrade logic: Can upgrade to higher tier OR longer billing cycle
- Downgrade: NOT allowed
- STARTER plan: Free forever, included by default

**Plan Tiers** (in ascending order):
- STARTER: Free plan
- PRO: $10/month | $108/year | $288/3-year
- PREMIUM: $30/month | $324/year | $864/3-year
- ENTERPRISE: Custom pricing (contact sales)

parameters.name: includeFeatures
parameters.description: If true, includes plan features in response. Default: false.

parameters.name: includeAllPlans
parameters.description: If true, returns all plans. If false, returns only upgradable plans. Default: true.

200.description: List of subscription plans with features

400.description: Invalid shopId format (must be valid ObjectId)

post.description:
Creates a Stripe Checkout session for subscription purchase or upgrade.

**Purchase**:
- Charges immediately
- Creates billing log: PURCHASE (PAID)

**Returning Customer**:
- Charges immediately
- Creates billing log: PURCHASE (PAID)
**Upgrade**:
- Calculates prorated credit from current subscription
- Applies credit to new plan cost
- Cancels previous subscription's upcoming renewal
- Creates new subscription with UPGRADE log

**Business Rules**:
- Downgrade: NOT allowed (must be higher tier or longer cycle)
- Admin activation: Can activate using shop credits

**Returns**: Stripe Checkout URL (redirect user to complete payment)

200.description: Stripe Checkout URL — redirect user to complete payment

400.description: Invalid plan, downgrade attempt, or insufficient credits

get.description:
Retrieves the shop's current active subscription with complete details.

**Authentication**: Requires valid JWT token. The shopId is extracted from the authenticated user's context.

**Response Includes**:
- Complete subscription details (ID, status, dates, payment info)
- Plan information (tier, duration, pricing, discount)
- Plan features with limits (e.g., "Max Products: 100", "API Calls: 10000/month")
- Action flags:
  - `canCancel`: Whether subscription can be cancelled
  - `canUpgrade`: Whether subscription can be upgraded
- Time information:
  - `daysRemaining`: Days until renewal or expiration (null for STARTER)

**Subscription Statuses**:
- `ACTIVE`: Normal active subscription, auto-renews if `isAutoRenew: true`
- `EXPIRING`: Cancelled but still valid until `endDate`, will not auto-renew

**STARTER Plan**:
- Free forever (no payment required)
- No expiration date (`endDate: null`)
- No billing logs (free plan)
- Cannot be cancelled (it's free)
- Can be upgraded to paid plans

**Use Cases**:
- Display current plan in merchant dashboard
- Show subscription status and renewal information
- Display available upgrade/cancel options
- Show days remaining until next billing

parameters.name: includeFeatures
parameters.description: If true, includes plan features in response. Default: false.

200.description: Current subscription details, or null if on STARTER/no active subscription

401.description: Invalid or missing authentication token

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of all subscriptions for the authenticated shop, ordered by most recent first.

**Includes all subscription states**:
- `ACTIVE`: Currently active subscription
- `EXPIRING`: Cancelled but still valid until end date
- `EXPIRED`: Past subscriptions that have ended
- `CANCELLED`: Manually cancelled subscriptions
- `TRIALING`: Trial subscriptions

**Use Cases**:
- Display full subscription history on the billing page
- Show all past and current plans in an admin panel

**Note**: STARTER plan subscriptions are included. Free plan entries will show `paidAmount: null`.

get.description:
Retrieves paginated billing history for the authenticated shop.

**Includes all billing events**:
- `PURCHASE`: New subscription purchased
- `UPGRADE`: Upgraded to higher plan/cycle
- `RENEWAL`: Auto-renewal processed
- `CANCELLATION`: Subscription cancelled
- `EXPIRATION`: Subscription expired

**Log Statuses**:
- `PAID`: Transaction completed
- `UPCOMING`: Scheduled for future (next renewal)
- `CANCEL`: Cancelled (will not process)

**Note**: STARTER plan has NO billing logs (free plan).

post.description:
Cancels the shop's current subscription (disables auto-renewal).

**Behavior**:
- Disables auto-renewal (`isAutoRenew = false`)
- Updates status to `EXPIRING`
- Cancels upcoming billing logs (UPCOMING â†’ CANCEL)
- Cancels Stripe subscription (if applicable)
- Shop retains access until `endDate`
- After `endDate`, reverts to STARTER plan

**Important**:
- Subscription remains ACTIVE until endDate
- Billing history is preserved (never deleted)
- No refunds for unused time (as per policy)
- Can reactivate later

**Note**: STARTER plan cannot be cancelled (it's free).

accessUntil.description: Date until which the shop retains access. Null for STARTER plan.

post.description:
Creates a new merchant account and shop using the same registration flow as POST /merchant/register.

**Key differences from regular registration:**
- Merchant is created with ACTIVE status (no email verification needed)
- Uses CreationSource.ADMIN context

**Two modes:**

1. **Minimal creation (email + password only):**
   - Merchant and shop are created
   - Shop name is derived from email prefix
   - Merchant completes onboarding on first login

2. **Full creation (email + password + shopName + shopUrl + optional logo):**
   - Merchant and shop are created
   - Shop setup is completed automatically (POST /shops/v2/setup is called internally)
   - Merchant skips onboarding on first login

**Note:** If shopName is provided, shopUrl must also be provided (and vice versa).

Minimal (requires onboarding).summary: Minimal creation - only email and password
Minimal (requires onboarding).description: Creates merchant and shop with minimal info. Merchant will need to complete onboarding on first login.

Full (skips onboarding).description: Creates merchant and shop with complete setup. Merchant skips onboarding on first login.
